2606.22608.title: Automated sign detection across the Electronic Babylonian Library: A large-scale dataset and end-to-end cuneiform OCR pipeline
2606.22608.paper_summary: Learning to read cuneiform tablets is an extremely demanding task; consequently, of the roughly half million excavated tablets, only a small fraction has been analysed by Assyriologists. Computer vision offers a promising avenue for decipherment but requires large, densely annotated datasets. To address this limitation, the largest annotated cuneiform sign dataset to date is used, and a Deformable Detection Transformer (DETR)-based object detection model is evaluated under two class granularities of 173 and 106 classes. The proposed system integrates automatic tablet-side extraction, heuristic line grouping, and n-gram-based textual similarity evaluation to bridge visual sign detection and textual structure, and achieves consistent improvements of up to 28-37% over prior work on COCO-style detection metrics. At inference, the method is applied to 87,668 tablet fragments from the Electronic Babylonian Library (eBL) corpus, producing nearly 2.9 million sign detections. Although the approach operates without linguistic priors and remains sensitive to tablet damage and layout variability, it provides a scalable and interpretable foundation for corpus-wide cuneiform analysis and supports future integration with multimodal and linguistic modelling frameworks.
2606.22608.paper_summary_zh: 學習閱讀楔形文字板是一項極具挑戰性的任務；因此，在大約五十萬個挖掘出的板塊中，只有一小部分被亞述學家分析。計算機視覺提供了一條有前景的解碼途徑，但需要大量密集標註的數據集。為了解決這一限制，使用了迄今為止最大的標註楔形文字符號數據集，並在173和106個類別的兩種類別粒度下評估基於可變形檢測Transformer（DETR）的物體檢測模型。所提出的系統整合了自動板邊提取、啟發式行分組和基於n-gram的文本相似性評估，以橋接視覺符號檢測和文本結構，並在COCO風格檢測指標上實現了高達28-37%的穩定改進。在推理階段，該方法應用於電子巴比倫圖書館（eBL）語料庫中的87,668個板塊碎片，產生了近290萬個符號檢測。儘管該方法在沒有語言先驗的情況下運行，並對板塊損壞和佈局變異保持敏感，但它為整個語料庫的楔形文字分析提供了一個可擴展且可解釋的基礎，並支持未來與多模態和語言建模框架的整合。
2606.22608.author: Wentao Che et.al.
2606.22608.authors: Wentao Che, Esteban Garcés Arias, Asim Niaz, Andreas Bender, Enrique Jiménez
